title: CONTRIBUTING: Document PR approval and link to OWNERS
body:
Remove a dead “Becoming a Maintainer” link and that stub section, since libpod currently doesn't document that process.  By leaving it undocumented, maintainer changes falls back to the usual pull-request review process.  I've added a link the `OWNERS` docs and our local `OWNERS` file so folks can discover the usual pull-request review process.  This portion of the PR is similar to kubernetes-incubator/cri-o#1272.

I've also updated `OWNERS` itself to rename `assignees` to `approvers`.  This is recommended in kubernetes/test-infra#3851, and is similar to what happened in kubernetes-incubator/cri-o#1103.

comments.body:
I'm not sure why Travis failed the integration tests.  Perhaps it was [some decreased logging][1]?

```
• Failure [7.319 seconds]
Podman logs
/go/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/test/e2e/logs_test.go:109
  podman logs tail 99 lines [It]
  /go/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/test/e2e/logs_test.go:69
  Expected
      <int>: 6
  to equal
      <int>: 4
```

I can't see how that would be related to this PR though.

[1]: https://travis-ci.org/projectatomic/libpod/jobs/372205548#L4645
Those tests are actually known (though infrequent) flakes - timing issues with running commands too close together in a potentially-slow travis environment
